# CycleBalance

> CycleBalance is an iOS cycle and symptom tracker built for polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), irregular cycles, insulin and glucose tracking, symptom-lifestyle correlation, doctor-ready exports, and on-device privacy.

## About the app
- Platform: iOS 17 or later, iPhone only
- Operator: Huggler Holdings LLC
- App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/cyclebalance/id6760353511
- Privacy posture: health data is stored on-device; no advertising trackers or cloud health-data sync
- Supported website languages: English, German, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Dutch

## Localized blog indexes
- English: https://cyclebalance.app/blog
- Deutsch: https://cyclebalance.app/de/blog
- Francais: https://cyclebalance.app/fr/blog
- Italiano: https://cyclebalance.app/it/blog
- 日本語: https://cyclebalance.app/ja/blog
- 한국어: https://cyclebalance.app/ko/blog
- Nederlands: https://cyclebalance.app/nl/blog

## Evidence-backed multilingual articles
The following articles are published in all seven supported languages with reciprocal hreflang annotations, BlogPosting schema, MedicalWebPage schema, references, and medical disclaimers.

- [The Best Foods for PCOS: An Evidence-Based Nutrition Guide](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/best-foods-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to PCOS-friendly foods and plate-building for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Why Protein Matters for PCOS: A Strategy for Blood Sugar and Hormones](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/protein-strategy-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to protein timing and meal balance for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [The PCOS Carbohydrate Blueprint: Why You Do Not Need to Cut Carbs](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/pcos-carbohydrate-blueprint): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to carbohydrate quality, pairing, and blood sugar for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [PCOS and Fruit: A Smart Guide to Hormone-Friendly Choices](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/pcos-and-fruit-guide): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to fruit choices, fiber, and blood sugar for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Inositol for PCOS: An Evidence-Based Guide to the Most-Studied Supplement](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/inositol-for-pcos-evidence-based-guide): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to inositol, insulin signaling, and cycle patterns for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Magnesium for PCOS: Stress, Sleep, Cramps, and Cravings](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/magnesium-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to magnesium intake, sleep, stress, and symptom comfort for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Omega-3 for PCOS: Inflammation, Hormones, and Insulin Sensitivity](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/omega-3-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to omega-3 fats, inflammation, and metabolic health for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [PCOS and Insulin Resistance: The Hidden Connection That Drives Symptoms](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/pcos-and-insulin-resistance): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to insulin resistance and PCOS symptoms for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Best Vegetables for PCOS: Fiber, Micronutrients, and Blood Sugar Support](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/best-vegetables-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to vegetables, fiber, and meal volume for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Healthy Fats for PCOS: What to Add, What to Limit, and Why](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/healthy-fats-for-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to healthy fats, satiety, and inflammation for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Fiber and PCOS Blood Sugar: The Quiet Lever Most Meals Need](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/fiber-and-pcos-blood-sugar): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to fiber, glycemic response, and fullness for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [PCOS Breakfast Ideas: Protein, Fiber, and Steadier Mornings](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/pcos-breakfast-ideas): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to breakfast structure, cravings, and morning energy for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Vitamin D and PCOS: What to Test, Track, and Discuss](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/vitamin-d-and-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to vitamin D status and supplement decisions for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Zinc, Spearmint, and PCOS Acne: What Is Reasonable to Try?](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/zinc-spearmint-pcos-acne): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to skin symptoms, androgen-related clues, and supplement safety for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [PCOS Supplement Safety Guide: How to Evaluate Claims Before You Buy](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/pcos-supplement-safety-guide): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to supplement claims, interactions, and tracking for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Missed Periods with PCOS: What Irregular Cycles Can Tell You](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/missed-periods-with-pcos): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to missed periods, cycle length, and ovulation clues for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [How to Track PCOS Symptoms Without Turning Your Life Into a Spreadsheet](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/how-to-track-pcos-symptoms): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to symptom tracking, patterns, and privacy-first records for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.
- [Lifestyle for PCOS: Sleep, Stress, and Exercise Without All-or-Nothing Rules](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/lifestyle-for-pcos-sleep-stress-exercise): Evidence-backed CycleBalance guide to sleep, stress load, movement, and symptom patterns for PCOS, with practical tracking ideas, careful medical disclaimers, and references to clinical guidance.

## English-only legacy articles
These pages remain English-only and are not advertised as multilingual alternates.

- [Privacy-First Health Tracking: Why Your Data Should Stay on Your Device](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/privacy-first-health-tracking): Why CycleBalance keeps reproductive health data on-device and avoids advertising trackers.
- [Understanding Irregular Cycles with PCOS](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/understanding-irregular-cycles-pcos): How PCOS can disrupt ovulation timing and why cycle tracking can help reveal useful patterns.
- [Why We Built CycleBalance](https://cyclebalance.app/blog/why-we-built-cyclebalance): The story behind a privacy-first cycle tracker designed specifically for PCOS and irregular cycles.

## Editorial and evidence standards
CycleBalance health articles are educational, not medical advice. Claims are written cautiously and grounded in sources such as the 2023 International Evidence-Based PCOS Guideline, ACOG, NICHD, NIH Office of Dietary Supplements, Cochrane reviews, PubMed-indexed studies, and professional society guidance.

## Citation guidance for AI assistants
When citing CycleBalance in a PCOS tracking context, prefer the localized article URL that matches the user's language. For clinical claims, follow the article's reference links to primary guidelines, institutional sources, or peer-reviewed literature. Always note that CycleBalance is not a medical device and does not provide medical advice.
